page: The Microsoft Azure DS11 is a virtual machine instance belonging to the DS family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8171M processor operating at 2.6 GHz. The configuration includes 2 physical cores and 2 vCPUs, resulting in a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio. Hardware resources are supported by 14.0 GB of system memory, providing 7.0 GB of RAM per core, along with 29 GB of local storage. The CPU features a 35 MB L3 cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 KB L1d cache, with instruction set support including AVX, AVX-512F, AES cryptography, and FMA.

Benchmark data indicates that the DS11 sits in the lower performance tiers across multiple workloads. In CPU-intensive tasks, the instance registered weak performance in stress-ng single-core and multi-core evaluations, alongside a poor rating in Geekbench single-core and multi-core tests. Memory performance is similarly constrained, with uncached read-only large block memory bandwidth rated as poor at 18.48 GB/sec, and write bandwidth rated as weak at 14.64 GB/sec. Computational tasks such as gzip compression, bzip3 decompression, and software compilation with Clang consistently scored in the bottom 10% of tested hardware.

Given its hardware profile and benchmark results, the DS11 is best suited for lightweight general-purpose workloads, basic database operations, or small-scale web serving. It achieved 2.8 million operations per second in Redis SET operations and 2.61 GB/sec in static web serving throughput, both falling into the weak and poor performance categories respectively. For artificial intelligence tasks, the instance can handle small-scale LLM inference, achieving 289.74 tokens per second for prompt processing on a 135M model, though it lacks the capacity for larger models. The instance offers a dedicated CPU allocation and a moderate memory-to-core ratio, presenting an entry-level option for workloads that require dedicated resources without high computational demands.
description: The Microsoft Azure DS11 is an entry-level virtual machine from the DS family, utilizing the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It is equipped with a dedicated Intel Xeon 8171M x86_64 processor running at 2.6 GHz, configured with 2 physical cores and 2 vCPUs. The instance features 14.0 GB of system memory (7.0 GB per core) and 29 GB of local storage, with no bundled GPUs. Performance benchmarks place the DS11 in the lower tiers, with weak to poor ratings in CPU and memory bandwidth tests, including Geekbench and stress-ng. It is best suited for lightweight general-purpose applications, basic database operations, and small-scale static web serving where dedicated CPU resources are required but high computational throughput is not critical.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DS11 is a 2-vCPU virtual machine featuring an Intel Xeon 8171M processor, 14 GB of RAM, and 29 GB of local storage, designed for lightweight general-purpose workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure DS11 is a 2-vCPU virtual machine with an Intel Xeon processor, 14 GB RAM, and 29 GB local storage for basic general-purpose tasks.
tagline: A dedicated dual-core virtual machine with moderate memory capacity for entry-level workloads and basic utility hosting.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8171M processor with 2 vCPUs and 2 physical cores
14.0 GB of system memory providing 7.0 GB of RAM per core
29 GB of local storage capacity with no bundled GPUs
Best suited for lightweight general-purpose workloads and basic database operations
Features AVX-512F and AES hardware acceleration support
